heading: Supplements to Federal grant programs
label: 15506 Supplements to Federal grant programs
notes: Codificationand enacted identical sections. was repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246
Effective DateSection effective on the first day of the first fiscal year beginning after , see , set out as a note under .June 18, 2008section 14217(d) of Pub. L. 110–246section 15101 of this title

children.text: they lack the economic resources to provide the required matching share; or
there are insufficient funds available under the applicable Federal law with respect to a project to be carried out in the region.
for any part of the basic Federal contribution to projects or activities under the Federal grant programs authorized by Federal laws; and
to increase the Federal contribution to projects and activities under the programs above the fixed maximum part of the cost of the projects or activities otherwise authorized by the applicable law.

children.heading: .—Certification Required
children.label: c .—Certification Required
children.text: For a program, project, or activity for which any part of the basic Federal contribution to the project or activity under a Federal grant program is proposed to be made under subsection (b), the Federal contribution shall not be made until the responsible Federal official administering the Federal law authorizing the Federal contribution certifies that the program, project, or activity meets the applicable requirements of the Federal law and could be approved for Federal contribution under that law if amounts were available under the law for the program, project, or activity.

children.heading: .—Limitations in Other Laws Inapplicable
children.label: d .—Limitations in Other Laws Inapplicable
children.text: Amounts provided pursuant to this subtitle are available without regard to any limitations on areas eligible for assistance or authorizations for appropriation in any other law.

children.heading: .—Federal Share
children.label: e .—Federal Share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of a project or activity receiving assistance under this section shall not exceed 80 percent.

children.heading: .—Maximum Commission Contribution
children.label: f .—Maximum Commission Contribution
children.text: Section 15501(d), relating to limitations on Commission contributions, shall apply to a program, project, or activity receiving assistance under this section.
